message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and the code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

LEVER: Journal-only retrieval + sidecar metadata enrichment (precondition lever) (L1)
RUBRIC CRITERION: "Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles'" (w=0.15 of instruction, dim w=0.25) + "Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized" (w=0.15 of comprehensiveness)
CHANGE: 66/97 cited sources are demonstrably not journal articles (personal homepages, a Substack, Goldman/JPM marketing, news, preprints) and the sidecar has authors on 4/105, venue on 32/105 with junk values ('Review of AI', year 2012 on a 2025 arXiv paper). Re-bank: (a) extend scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py with a Crossref/DOI-resolution pass to populate authors[]/venue/year on every T1/T2 row; (b) re-tier and DEMOTE grey literature to a lunon-style signpost convention ('named and located in the literature but never cited as evidence for a load-bearing claim') rather than delete; (c) add journal-targeted retrieval queries so the evidence base is majority peer-reviewed. This unlocks L2/L3 which are otherwise dishonest or fabrication.
WHERE: retrieval — /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py (extend with Crossref pass), corpus /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json rebuild; audit seam at scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:104 (_audit_citations)
CLAIMED: +0.010 to +0.015 overall alone (instruction 0.4409 vs cellcog 0.5530 is a 0.028-overall gap; this captures the sourcing half of it only in combination with L2/L3)
ADMITTED FAILURE: Crossref misses non-DOI items so attribution coverage stays ~30% and L2 starves; or journal-only filtering guts sector coverage and fights L4; or enrichment writes wrong venues — which is fabrication injected into prose downstream.
CONTRACT: Two-lane contract, zero-fabrication preserved by construction. LANE A (world-facts): unchanged — every sentence asserting a fact about the world carries [ev_XXX] tokens and passes verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098) with the NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) intact. LANE B (adjudication): a sentence tagged [syn] by the writer is exempt from span entailment and admitted iff it introduces NO new fact — enforced by a new verify_synthesis_sentence beside the existing gate: (i) DETERMINISTIC closed-world check: every digit-bearing token, proper noun, and named mechanism in the [syn] sentence must already appear verbatim in this section's ADMITTED lane-A prose or the bibliography (cellcog's winning synthesis — 'The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive... layered lenses to be deployed according to the question at hand' — passes this check by inspection: no new number, entity, or fact; only ranking/relating ideas already on the page); (ii) FAIL-CLOSED LLM check: 'does this sentence assert any empirical claim about the world not already asserted in the FACTS block above, or does it only rank, relate, reconcile, or evaluate them?' — any parse error, timeout, or uncertain verdict = drop the sentence. LANE B-NEG (negative-space claims): sentences of the form 'no peer-reviewed study in this corpus has yet X' are claims about the CORPUS, not the world, and are verified mechanically against the evidence pool + bibliography (they are also immune to the NEUTRAL clause by construction — they introduce no mechanism into any span). ATTRIBUTION: author/venue strings injected into prose must be byte-substrings of the ENRICHED sidecar record for the cited evidence_id (extending _audit_citations at compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:104), and the sidecar itself is spot-verified against source pages before any compose — the current sidecar fabricates venues ('Review of AI', wrong years) and MUST NOT be injected until repaired. Red-team acceptance: a 30-sentence set (15 genuine adjudications, 15 with one smuggled fact/number/entity each) must show >=12/15 genuine admitted and 0/15 smuggled admitted, else Lane B ships disabled and we fall back to lane-A-only compose.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
JUDGE-INVISIBILITY: RACE deletes all [n] markers and the reference list, then scores our report SIDE BY SIDE against a 0.5102 reference in ONE call. Would the judge SEE this, and would it push a criterion from the "6-8 Good" band into "8-10 Excellent"? There is no credit for being a better 7.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

/home/polaris/polaris_project/task72_prompt.txt:1:Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt:2:Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt:11:- [w=0.15] Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: Evaluates if the review synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles), covering the main research themes, findings, and ongoing debates regarding AI's impact on the labor market. This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt:16:- [w=0.25] Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: Evaluates the review's ability to critically synthesize diverse findings from high-quality journal articles to present a nuanced understanding of AI's 'significant disruptions.' This includes identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt:27:- [w=0.15] Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' :: Verifies that all cited sources are academic, peer-reviewed journal articles (or meet a clear standard of 'high-quality' if defined contextually, e.g., leading journals), and that other forms of literature (e.g., books, conference proceedings, news articles, blogs, non-peer-reviewed reports) are not cited, adhering to the 'only cites high-quality... journal articles' constraint.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt:28:- [w=0.10] Exclusive Citation of 'English-Language' Journal Articles :: Ensures that all journal articles cited in the review are originally published in English or are established English translations, strictly adhering to the language constraint for sources.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt:34:- [w=0.15] P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information :: Assesses how clearly, concisely, and accurately key arguments, findings, theories, and methodologies from the cited journal articles are presented. Evaluates the effectiveness of synthesizing information from multiple sources to build a coherent understanding of topics (e.g., AI's effect on job displacement vs. creation, or changes in skill demands across industries), rather than just serially summarizing individual papers. Checks for appropriate information density and avoidance of redundancy.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/brief_v3_bundle.txt:45:  articles."* **We cite 97 journal articles. The judge sees zero.** Our instruction-following: 0.4409. cellcog: 0.5530.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/brief_v3_bundle.txt:117:**Methods.** The review draws on approximately 96 English-language peer-reviewed journal articles from economics, management and operations, sociology and technology studies, and applied health and education venues, spanning the early 1990s through April 2026. Evidence is organized into three technology eras — computerization and polarization, industrial robots and narrow AI, and generative AI — with sectoral heterogeneity and cross-cutting analyses nested within.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/brief_v3_bundle.txt:137:This review is restricted by design to peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles. The corpus was assembled through iterative database searches across economics journals (*Quarterly Journal of Economics*, *American Economic Review*, *Journal of Political Economy*, *Journal of Labor Economics*, *Journal of Economic Perspectives*, *European Economic Review*, *Review of Economic Dynamics*, *Structural Change and Economic Dynamics*), management and operations journals (*Management Science*, *Organization Science*, *International Journal of Production Economics*, *Industry and Innovation*, *Strategic Management Journal*), sociology and technology-studies journals (*Work, Employment and Society*, *New Technology, Work and Employment*, *Organization Studies*, *Human Relations*, *Technological Forecasting and Social Change*, *Technology in Society*, *Social Forces*), and applied health and education journals (*JAMA Network Open*, *Cogent Education*) where sector-specific evidence is relevant. Approximately ninety-six articles comprise the core evidence base.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/brief_v3_bundle.txt:177:A complementary mechanism-level framework has been developed by Agrawal, Gans, and Goldfarb across a sequence of peer-reviewed journal articles. Their central move is to define recent advances in machine learning economically as a reduction in the cost of producing *predictions* — that is, the inference of unobserved states from available information. Prediction is valuable because it is an input into decision-making, not because it is the whole decision itself. In their 2018 *AEA Papers and Proceedings* piece, they formalize *judgment* as the human task of discovering the payoffs attached to action-state pairs. Their 2019 *Journal of Economic Perspectives* article (Agrawal, Gans, & Goldfarb, 2019a) translates the framework into task-level labor implications: cheaper prediction can automate prediction tasks themselves, automate complementary decision tasks when machine action becomes relatively more valuable, augment human decision-makers when better prediction raises the productivity of human judgment, or create new decision tasks by making previously uneconomic activities viable. Agrawal, Gans, and Goldfarb (2019b) embed the framework in formal decision-making under uncertainty, and their 2024 *Journal of Economics & Management Strategy* article traces the system-wide organizational redesign required when tasks are interdependent. The framework complements Acemoglu–Restrepo by specifying *which* tasks tend to be automated — those reducible to prediction — and which remain complementary to labor: those requiring judgment, contextual integration, or ethical evaluation.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:121:- 2026-07-13 02:15 | **THE FINDING THAT MATTERS MOST TONIGHT — AND IT IS THE OPERATOR'S IDEA, WHICH I KILLED AT 21:55: TIER COMPLIANCE COLLAPSED AS THE REPORT GREW.** Cited-entry T1-T3 share: **CTRL 57.8% -> RANK7 53.7% -> RANK9 45.4% -> RANK10 43.3%.** DRB task 72 instructs *"only cite high-quality, English-language journal articles"* — so **the depth push made INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING WORSE**, and this metric is **deterministic (no noise)**. Rank10 cites a **Toptal freelancer listicle, an amplifai blog, apporya.com, a Substack, hrexecutive.com**, while **112 T1-T3 works that survived the judge are never cited at all.**
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:138:  - **ALL BIBLIOGRAPHY-SIDE WORK SCORES ZERO.** The source-eligibility firewall (DOI/journal/tier metadata, mirror dedup, purging Toptal/blog entries) is INVISIBLE to the judge. The instruction-following criterion "Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles" (w=0.15) can ONLY be reached via **venue names written in the running prose**. (The 0.5000 reference ships ZERO inline citations and no bibliography at all.)
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:168:- 2026-07-13 06:40 | **WHERE THEY DISAGREE — AND ARM 2 IS THE EXPERIMENT THAT SETTLES IT.** **Sol: the residual is in the EVIDENCE** — we name WEF/Goldman Sachs/BLS/vendor forecasts IN PROSE (these SURVIVE the cleaner), so the judge sees a review violating its own "journal articles only" instruction; fix the bank => 0.535-0.552. **Fable: the residual is in the GENERATION** — you cannot retrofit an argument onto an extraction mosaic; bodhi's prose was AUTHORED as adjudicated synthesis at 59w/paragraph, so wave 2 must be a composer that writes that way natively at ~5k words. **Both are probably right; Arm 2 discriminates at ZERO compose cost.**
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:175:- 2026-07-13 07:10 | **THE CHEAPEST LEVER ON THE BOARD — AND BOTH DESIGNERS WALKED PAST IT.** RACE deletes every `[n]` marker and the whole reference list before the judge reads a word. **We put ALL our sourcing into 240 `[n]` markers => THE JUDGE SEES NO SOURCES AT ALL.** cellcog writes *"Acemoglu and Restrepo (2018), in the* American Economic Review*..."* — **ordinary prose the cleaner cannot touch.** Measured in-prose attribution: **cellcog 133 author-year (9.8/1k words) + 65 journal names; WhaleCloud 66 (9.5/1k); POLARIS 10 (1.3/1k) + 1 journal name.** On a task whose instruction is literally *"only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles"*: **we cite 97 journal articles and the judge sees ZERO.** (Our instruction-following 0.4409 vs cellcog 0.5530.) **We ALREADY HOLD authors+venues in bibliography.json — the fix is a deterministic, fabrication-proof transform.**

content.content:
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:10:- 2026-07-12 | FABLE GATE | Verdict PASS-WITH-FIX. Confirmed no dangling-ev_id bug (outline plans predate the seam but every consumer is membership-guarded; bibliography only draws from actually-cited rows). Found a latent hard-drop I missed → Rank2b.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:52:- The 4 sciencedirect rows share the stale "Just a moment..." TITLE but have genuinely recovered bodies — 3 are correctly KEPT, but their **title is still chrome** and will render into the bibliography as "Just a moment...". Title repair (NOT deletion) is a separate small step.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:74:- 2026-07-12 22:15 | **THE RELEVANCE PAYOFF IS ~ZERO — MY OWN 21:40 THESIS IS DEAD.** I argued (and Fable half-endorsed) that a ~51%-alien menu poisons shipped prose, so the clean pool's prize is RELEVANCE, i.e. a RACE win. **Measured it instead of assuming: the dirty report's shipped bibliography is 1.6% alien — ONE row (ev_852) out of 63.** The armed report: 0 of 72. **A ~51%-alien POOL yields a ~1.6%-alien BIBLIOGRAPHY** — the section writer was already discarding alien rows at citation time. So the relevance prize is worth *one citation*. Both the "poisoned prose" story and the RACE-relevance payoff are **refuted by the data**, and they were mine.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:77:- 2026-07-12 22:15 | Rank6a LEAK MEASURED, NOT ASSUMED: armed cites **0 off-subject rows** — the back-door fold-in did not re-import any judged-alien row into the shipped bibliography this run. The hole is still structurally real (`dropped_rows=[]` hard-coded) and must be armed before any retrieval-side depth push, but it is **not currently leaking into the report**. Downgraded from "load-bearing now" to "latent, gated on the next retrieval increase".
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:82:- 2026-07-12 22:30 | FABLE ON CLAIM 1 (relevance ~zero): **CONFIRMED — independently recomputed from raw files, 1.6% stands.** But **two latent holes in my method that happened not to bite**, and they must be on the record: (i) my `ev_\d+` regex over report.md was **structurally vacuous** — prose cites with `[n]` markers, and both runs record `leaked_cite_ev_tokens: 0`, so that leg could never have matched anything; (ii) corpus ids are **zero-padded** (`ev_050`), so a raw string intersection silently misses unpadded variants. The answer survived only because the bibliography `evidence_id` leg carried it. **A measurement that returns the right answer through a dead code path is not a measurement.**
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:83:- 2026-07-12 22:30 | **NEW DEFECT, FOUND BY FABLE, SHIPPED IN THE *CLEAN* ARM: the gap-fill rows the judge never sees are entering the bibliography as LEAD citations, and they are garbage.** ARMED cites 3 unjudged fresh rows (ev_1283/1290/1291; CTRL cites 1). Bibliography entry **#1 = ev_1290**, whose ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED/SUPPORTS `claim_text` is a **chopped reference-list scrap** ("on and sustainable growth. _Technol. Forecast. Soc. Change_ 162, 120398..."); entry **#2 = ev_1291** has `basket_verdict: "unverified"` (weight 0.45) and is **cited in prose anyway**. So the clean report's two lead citations are junk that bypassed the judge via the Rank6a fold-in hole. My relevance metric is **blind to this** — it is a *quality* failure, not a topicality one, and unlike the alien-row problem **it actually shipped.** This is now the strongest known defect in the output.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:87:- 2026-07-12 23:15 | RANK8's HIDDEN COST (measured, not assumed): the cap buys depth partly by **narrowing breadth** — bibliography **45 entries vs 63 (CTRL) / 72 (ARMED)** — and by leaning on **8 unjudged gap-fill rows** (vs 1 in CTRL), i.e. exactly the unscreened class Fable caught shipping as garbage lead citations. **The cap and the Rank6a hole compound each other.** So cap=15 is NOT free: it trades RACE coverage for depth. Do not ship it naked.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:88:- 2026-07-12 23:20 | **RANK7 LANDED — THE DEPTH CEILING WAS ONE PROMPT LINE, AND IT BROKE: 3408 -> 7723 WORDS (2.27x).** Clean pool + `PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET=28-36` + `PG_SECTION_DISTINCT_SOURCES=15`, first end-to-end run of the Rank7 lever. **187 verified sentences (vs 81)** and — the number that matters most against the padding hypothesis — **107 bibliography entries vs 63**: it got LONGER by citing MORE DISTINCT SOURCES, which is precisely what rule #9 was designed to force (B11 same-span dedup deletes re-worded sentences that re-cite an already-used span, so length CANNOT be bought by restatement of the same span). Quality held: **2 alien citations (1.9%), 4 unjudged fresh rows, and ZERO `basket_verdict=unverified` entries** — the exact defect Fable caught shipping in the ARMED arm did **not** recur.
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:105:- 2026-07-13 00:25 | **DENSITY INSTRUMENT BUILT — AND IT CAUGHT ITS OWN BUG BEFORE I SHIPPED A NUMBER** (`scripts/flywheel_density.py`). Measures Fable's pre-registered falsifier: **distinct WORKS (not bib entries) per 1000 BODY words**, collapsing mirrors via the corpus's own `same_work_groups` -> DOI -> normalized title. **The bug: `report.md` ENDS WITH THE NUMBERED `## References` LIST**, so a marker regex over the whole file re-reads the bibliography as prose and reports **every entry as cited** — the tell was `cited == bib` for all four arms. Fixed to body-only (both numerator and denominator). **Corrected, it independently reproduces Fable's hand-count: 82 cited markers in rank7 (Fable counted 82), 77 distinct works (Fable estimated ~73).** Two independent methods converging is the strongest check available here. *This is the second measurement of mine tonight that returned a plausible number through a dead code path — the pattern is now the thing to watch, not the individual bug.*
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:120:- 2026-07-13 02:15 | **FABLE GATE #3 — THE 4th ARTIFACT, AND IT WAS MY OWN INSTRUMENT FLATTERING THE ARM I WAS SELLING.** Fable re-keyed my density metric through the corpus rows and got **68 works @ 8.78/1k** where I reported **94 @ 12.14**. I verified it: **`flywheel_density.py` read `title`, but the bibliography key is `source_title`** — so every title normalized to `""`, the fallback keyed each entry by its own ev_id, and **NOT ONE MIRROR WAS EVER COLLAPSED.** Worse, when I fixed that, a *second* ordering bug surfaced: **DOI-before-title means a work WITH a doi never meets its doi-LESS mirror** — `[1]` Acemoglu-Restrepo (T1, AEA JEP, DOI) and `[13]`, the IZA `[PDF]` scrape of the *same paper* (T4, no DOI), counted as **two works**. Same for "Generative AI at Work" `[42]` vs its arXiv mirror `[78]`. **No single key identifies a work — the mirrors disagree about which field they carry.** Rebuilt on **UNION-FIND** (merge on same_work_group OR doi OR title-prefix). Honest numbers: CTRL **36 works @ 14.47/1k**, RANK10 **86 @ 11.11/1k**. Still less aggressive than Fable's 68, but **the direction and every conclusion are identical.** *The error inflated the LONG arms specifically — they cite more mirrors. An instrument whose bias points at your own hypothesis is the most dangerous kind, and this is the FOURTH time tonight a measurement of mine returned a plausible number through a broken path.*
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:124:- 2026-07-13 02:15 | **RANK12 LANDED IN CODE + LAUNCHED: `_tier_first_menu()` (`PG_WRITER_MENU_TIER_FIRST`, default-OFF).** STABLE-sorted, applied **BEFORE** the cap takes its head-N (reordering *after* truncation would only shuffle rows already chosen — a silent no-op, the exact failure class that has bitten me three times tonight). **Stable** so rows keep relevance order WITHIN a tier: this raises quality **among equals**, it does **not** re-rank on quality INSTEAD of relevance — an off-topic T1 row is still off-topic, and the **topic judge remains the instrument for that**. WRITER PROMPT ONLY: `ev_ids`, `evidence_pool`, bibliography and the frozen faithfulness engine untouched; every sentence still gated against the FULL pool. Unknown/missing tiers sort **LAST**, never raise. **11 tests**, incl. the one that counts: cap alone fills the 2 slots with **T4/T7** (defect reproduced); tier-first + cap fills them with **T1/T2** (fix). Running now alongside Rank11 (repetition guard — Fable predicts it catches **0-2**, since it is EXACT-match and every duplicate found was a PARAPHRASE).
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:138:  - **ALL BIBLIOGRAPHY-SIDE WORK SCORES ZERO.** The source-eligibility firewall (DOI/journal/tier metadata, mirror dedup, purging Toptal/blog entries) is INVISIBLE to the judge. The instruction-following criterion "Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles" (w=0.15) can ONLY be reached via **venue names written in the running prose**. (The 0.5000 reference ships ZERO inline citations and no bibliography at all.)
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:149:- 2026-07-13 03:20 | **RANK12 (SOURCE-ELIGIBILITY FIREWALL) SCORED: 0.4276 — SCORE-NEUTRAL. THE DRIVER'S "CLEANEST WIN OF THE NIGHT" WAS WORTH NOTHING.** comprehens 0.4562 | insight 0.4133 | instr-follow 0.4280 | readability 0.3979. It sits INSIDE the Rank9/10/11 noise band (0.4133-0.4313). **The tell: the firewall was aimed at INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING (T1-T3 bibliography compliance 43.3% -> 72.8%, +29.5 pts) and instruction-following on the SCORECARD went 0.4409 (Rank10) -> 0.4280 — DOWN, within noise. The lever aimed at the dimension did not move the dimension.** Exactly as the ArticleCleaner finding predicts: the judge never sees our citations, so citation quality cannot score. **THIRD independent confirmation, at zero extra cost: length -> no movement; padding removal -> no movement; source compliance -> no movement. EVERY lever pulled since Rank7 has been INVISIBLE TO THE SCORER.**
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:162:- 2026-07-13 04:10 | STATE: SOTA=0.5265 | us=0.4382 | **gap 0.088**. Wave1 (structure + interpretive weave) projects 0.46-0.48; Wave2 (comparison matrix + sectoral + in-prose venue naming) projects 0.47-0.50. **Neither reaches SOTA on current projections and that is on the record BEFORE we build, not after.**
/home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:175:- 2026-07-13 07:10 | **THE CHEAPEST LEVER ON THE BOARD — AND BOTH DESIGNERS WALKED PAST IT.** RACE deletes every `[n]` marker and the whole reference list before the judge reads a word. **We put ALL our sourcing into 240 `[n]` markers => THE JUDGE SEES NO SOURCES AT ALL.** cellcog writes *"Acemoglu and Restrepo (2018), in the* American Economic Review*..."* — **ordinary prose the cleaner cannot touch.** Measured in-prose attribution: **cellcog 133 author-year (9.8/1k words) + 65 journal names; WhaleCloud 66 (9.5/1k); POLARIS 10 (1.3/1k) + 1 journal name.** On a task whose instruction is literally *"only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles"*: **we cite 97 journal articles and the judge sees ZERO.** (Our instruction-following 0.4409 vs cellcog 0.5530.) **We ALREADY HOLD authors+venues in bibliography.json — the fix is a deterministic, fabrication-proof transform.**

content.content:
logging.basicConfig(
    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
    stream=sys.stdout,
)
for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
log = logging.getLogger("compose")


def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
    from collections import Counter
    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}


# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")


def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.

    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
    return {
        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
    }


# ── FLYWHEEL Rank4 — ARM THE SEMANTIC TOPIC JUDGE ON A PRE-BUILT CORPUS ─────────────────────────
#
# THE GAP (measured, not theorised): the §-1.3.1(b) off-topic DELETE leg has never fired on this
# path. `is_row_deletable_offtopic` keys on an AFFIRMATIVE ``topic_off_subject`` stamp, and the ONLY
# writer of that stamp is ``classify_topic_relevance`` — which runs at RETRIEVAL time (the live
# sweep) and is called by NOTHING on the pre-built-corpus path. The corpus carries 633
# ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` labels but ZERO ``topic_off_subject`` stamps, so the run logged
# "DELETED 0 judge-confirmed off-topic item(s)" and ~294 alien sources (climate-risk IMF working
# papers, privacy-attack bibliographies, school-infrastructure pages, content-marketing stats) sat in
# the grounding pool, eligible to be CITED in an AI-labour literature review. The faithfulness engine
# cannot catch them: strict_verify checks span fidelity, not topicality — a sentence grounded on a
# climate working paper is perfectly FAITHFUL and perfectly off-topic.
#
# THE FIX: ARM the judge that already exists. We do NOT build a new one, and we do NOT lexically
# guess. §-1.3.1(b) admits exactly one delete trigger — an affirmative SEMANTIC judge verdict,

EOF
